metadata_information.title: Local Economic Shocks and Human Capital Accumulation : Evidence from Rwandan Coffee Mills
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Local Economic Shocks and Human Capital Accumulation : Evidence from Rwandan Coffee Mills

document_description.abstract: This paper examines the medium-term effects of policy- driven income shocks on human capital accumulation in low-income environments. Using administrative data on test scores of the universe of primary school students in Rwanda and the staggered rollout of coffee mills in the country, it shows a positive spillover effect of the coffee mills on students’ performance. Early life exposure to coffee mills is associated with a 0.09 standard deviation (4 percent) increase in student test scores. Improvements in household welfare, child health, and school attendance are likely operative channels of impact.

An objective of the series is to get the findings out quickly, even if the presentations are less than fully polished. The papers carry the names of the authors and should be cited accordingly.
